refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-006 (Refined): The NGHE solution shall generate and maintain a real-time worksite model representing terrain elevation, static obstacles (rocks, structures, vegetation), dynamic hazards (personnel, other equipment), and work-zone boundaries, with sufficient fidelity to support autonomous path planning and operator situational awareness.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was vague on fidelity requirements, update frequency, and use-case binding. Refinement clarifies that the model must serve both planning (offline) and execution (real-time) functions, must distinguish static from dynamic features, and must be safety-critical (high criticality tailoring). This enables downstream DP selection to address sensing, fusion, and distribution architecture.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Acceptance test: (1) Worksite model generated within 5 minutes of site entry; (2) Terrain elevation accuracy ±0.5 m over 95% of mapped area; (3) Obstacle detection sensitivity ≥95% for objects >0.3 m; (4) Model available to planning and execution subsystems via agreed interface; (5) Safety-critical features (hazard zones) marked with confidence metadata.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-006.1: Acquire worksite sensor data (LiDAR, camera, IMU) from vehicle platform.
CR-NGHE-006.2: Fuse multi-modal sensor data into unified 3D terrain and obstacle representation.
CR-NGHE-006.3: Classify and label worksite features (terrain, static obstacles, dynamic hazards, work zones).
CR-NGHE-006.4: Distribute worksite model to planning, execution, and operator interfaces with latency <2 s.
CR-NGHE-006.5: Validate model completeness and confidence; flag gaps and low-confidence regions to Safety Services.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Onboard sensor fusion (LiDAR + stereo camera + IMU) on vehicle; local 3D reconstruction; periodic upload to cloud backend for archival and planning access.
DP-B: Distributed sensing: vehicle-mounted primary sensors + fixed site beacons/markers; centralized fusion in Safety Services; model broadcast to all consumers.
DP-C: Hybrid edge-cloud: vehicle performs real-time local fusion for execution; Safety Services maintains authoritative model for planning and audit; bidirectional sync via Telemetry interface.
DP-D: Tele-operator-directed mapping: operator manually annotates video feed; Safety Services aggregates annotations into model; low automation, high operator workload.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Hybrid edge-cloud architecture with vehicle-side real-time fusion for execution safety and Safety Services-side authoritative model for planning, coordination, and safety validation. Model synchronization via Telemetry interface; operator and planning systems access via Tasking and SafetyAlert interfaces.

potential_sources: Vehicle sensor suite (LiDAR, camera, IMU) must be specified in parallel with fusion algorithm; tight coupling on sensor calibration and timing.
Safety Services model validation logic must align with vehicle-side confidence thresholds; risk of divergence if not co-designed.
Telemetry bandwidth and latency constraints may limit model update frequency; must be negotiated early.
coupling_check.notes: DP-C decouples execution (vehicle) from planning (Safety Services) via asynchronous Telemetry sync, reducing real-time coupling. However, sensor specification and fusion algorithm are tightly coupled; recommend parallel decomposition of vehicle sensing subsystem.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Vehicle platform has sufficient onboard compute (GPU/FPGA) for real-time 3D fusion; risk if compute budget exhausted by other functions.
Assumption: Telemetry interface supports model delta updates (not full model re-transmission); risk of bandwidth saturation if full model sent every cycle.
Assumption: Safety Services can validate model completeness within planning cycle time (e.g., 10 min); risk if validation is slow, delaying plan approval.
Risk: Operator situational awareness depends on model latency; if Telemetry sync lags >2 s, operator may make decisions on stale data.
Risk: High safety criticality requires model confidence metadata and failure modes analysis; DP-C must include explicit confidence propagation and anomaly detection.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-C to explicitly specify: (1) Sensor fusion algorithm (e.g., EKF, particle filter, neural network); (2) Confidence/uncertainty representation (e.g., covariance, Bayesian posterior); (3) Telemetry sync protocol (delta vs. full, frequency, compression); (4) Safety Services validation rules (e.g., require >90% confidence for hazard zones); (5) Fallback behavior if model diverges between vehicle and Safety Services.

im_boundary.name: Safety Services
im_boundary.scope: Authoritative worksite model repository, model fusion/validation logic, safety-critical feature labeling, model distribution to planning and operator interfaces, anomaly detection and confidence assessment.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle sends raw/fused sensor data and local model snapshots; Safety Services sends validated model and confidence metadata back to vehicle for execution feedback.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Safety Services distributes worksite model to planning subsystem (Site Manager, System Engineer) to inform mission planning and task allocation.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety Services flags model gaps, low-confidence regions, and detected hazards to Safety Manager and operator for situational awareness and intervention.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Safety Services communicates with Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems over network; model updates and telemetry flow bidirectionally.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the top-level system; worksite modeling is a core capability but not the sole responsibility. Assigning to NGHE Solution would diffuse ownership and make interface traceability unclear. Not recommended as primary IM.

Vehicle can host real-time sensor fusion for execution, but authoritative model validation and planning-level distribution are outside vehicle scope. Vehicle is a data producer, not the model authority. Partial containment only.

Teleoperation is a consumer of worksite model (for operator situational awareness) but not the producer or validator. Assigning model authority to Teleoperation would create circular dependency with Vehicle and Safety Services. Not recommended.

Safety Services is the natural authority for safety-critical model validation, hazard labeling, and distribution to planning/execution/operator. High containment: model validation, confidence assessment, anomaly detection, and safety-critical feature labeling are core Safety Services responsibilities. Low diffusion: clear ownership boundary, minimal interface leakage.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Safety Services owns the authoritative worksite model, including fusion validation, confidence assessment, hazard detection, and distribution to planning (Tasking), execution (Telemetry), and operator (SafetyAlert). Vehicle owns real-time local fusion for execution safety; Safety Services owns model authority and planning-level distribution. This separation of concerns (edge execution vs. cloud authority) is architecturally sound for safety-critical systems.
containment_risks: Risk: If Safety Services model validation is slow, planning may be delayed; recommend asynchronous validation with provisional model release.
Risk: Telemetry bandwidth may limit model update frequency; recommend delta-based sync and compression to mitigate.
Risk: Operator situational awareness depends on SafetyAlert latency; if Safety Services is geographically remote, latency may exceed 2 s; recommend local caching of model on Teleoperation subsystem.
Risk: Vehicle and Safety Services models may diverge if Telemetry sync fails; recommend explicit reconciliation protocol and fallback to vehicle-local model for execution.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify IM boundary: Safety Services owns model authority and planning-level distribution; Vehicle owns real-time local fusion for execution. Define explicit sync protocol (Telemetry interface) with conflict resolution rules. Recommend adding a local model cache in Teleoperation subsystem to reduce SafetyAlert latency for operator situational awareness.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C (hybrid edge-cloud) directly addresses refined FR: vehicle-side fusion supports real-time execution; Safety Services-side model supports planning and safety validation. DP-C is traceable to all sub-FRs (sensing, fusion, classification, distribution, validation). No functional gaps identified.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion risk: Vehicle sensor specification and fusion algorithm are tightly coupled; recommend parallel decomposition of Vehicle sensing subsystem to avoid diffusion.

Diffusion risk: Telemetry interface must support both raw sensor data (for Safety Services fusion) and fused model (for vehicle execution); recommend explicit protocol definition to avoid interface creep.
Diffusion risk: Safety Services model validation logic must be decoupled from vehicle-side fusion; recommend clear API contract (e.g., model schema, confidence format) to avoid tight coupling.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Safety Services containment score 0.9 is high; ownership is clear. Vehicle is a data producer (0.5 containment); Teleoperation is a consumer (0.3 containment). Interface boundaries are well-defined via Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Network Link. Recommend explicit interface specifications to prevent diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible: (1) Model generation latency can be measured end-to-end (sensor to Safety Services); (2) Terrain accuracy can be validated against ground truth survey; (3) Obstacle detection can be tested in controlled site scenarios; (4) Interface availability can be verified via integration testing; (5) Safety-critical feature labeling can be audited via Safety Services logs.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: Safety Services owns model authority and validation; Vehicle owns real-time fusion; Teleoperation owns operator interface. Each module has distinct responsibilities and interfaces. Recommend documenting allocation in system architecture document.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-C to specify fusion algorithm, confidence representation, and Telemetry sync protocol (see DP revision above).
Define explicit interface contracts: (1) Telemetry schema for sensor data and model updates; (2) Tasking schema for model distribution to planning; (3) SafetyAlert schema for hazard notifications.
Clarify fallback behavior if Safety Services model validation fails or Telemetry sync is lost; recommend vehicle-local model as fallback for execution.
Recommend adding local model cache in Teleoperation subsystem to reduce operator situational awareness latency.
Recommend parallel decomposition of Vehicle sensing subsystem (LiDAR, camera, IMU) to avoid tight coupling with fusion algorithm.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-006 is not atomic; refined FR identifies 5 sub-FRs (sensing, fusion, classification, distribution, validation). DP-C is sound and traceable to refined FR. Safety Services IM assignment is well-justified (0.9 containment). However, sub-FRs require further decomposition to specify sensor suite, fusion algorithm, classification logic, and distribution protocol. Recommend decomposing each sub-FR to DP and IM level before detailed design.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-006.1: Acquire worksite sensor data (LiDAR, camera, IMU) from vehicle platform.
CR-NGHE-006.2: Fuse multi-modal sensor data into unified 3D terrain and obstacle representation.
CR-NGHE-006.3: Classify and label worksite features (terrain, static obstacles, dynamic hazards, work zones).
CR-NGHE-006.4: Distribute worksite model to planning, execution, and operator interfaces with latency <2 s.
CR-NGHE-006.5: Validate model completeness and confidence; flag gaps and low-confidence regions to Safety Services.

assumptions: Vehicle platform has sufficient onboard compute (GPU/FPGA) for real-time 3D fusion.
Telemetry interface supports model delta updates and bidirectional sync.
Safety Services can validate model completeness within planning cycle time.
Operator situational awareness latency requirement is <2 s.
High safety criticality requires explicit confidence metadata and anomaly detection.
Worksite features (terrain, obstacles, hazards) are distinguishable via multi-modal sensing.
open_risks: Vehicle-Safety Services model divergence if Telemetry sync fails; requires reconciliation protocol.
Operator situational awareness latency if Safety Services is geographically remote; recommend local caching.
Telemetry bandwidth saturation if full model re-transmitted every cycle; recommend delta-based sync.
Safety Services validation bottleneck if model validation is slow; recommend asynchronous validation.
Sensor calibration and timing coupling between Vehicle and Safety Services; requires parallel decomposition.
evidence_needed: Sensor specification (LiDAR range/resolution, camera FOV/resolution, IMU accuracy) for Vehicle subsystem.
Fusion algorithm selection (EKF, particle filter, neural network) with performance benchmarks.
Confidence representation scheme (covariance, Bayesian posterior, other) with propagation rules.
Telemetry interface specification (schema, sync protocol, compression, frequency).
Safety Services validation rules (confidence thresholds, anomaly detection logic, hazard labeling criteria).
Operator situational awareness latency measurements in field trials.
Ground truth survey data for terrain accuracy validation.
